channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The Scam Campaign Lifecycle: Message, Trust, Infrastructure, Action
item.description: Most scam analysis begins too late. A suspicious website is found, a fake profile is reported, a phone number is flagged, or a victim submits a screenshot after the damage has already started. By...

item.title: More ‘Kryten’ and Less ‘Replicant’: The Case for the Mechanical Tell
item.description: In Star Trek, why does the computer sound so mechanical and emotionless when the holodeck can create characters who can converse indistinguishably from humans? In Voyager, the Doctor himself is as...

item.title: How to Use WireGuard 2.0 with Kubernetes 1.38 for Secure Cluster Networking
item.description: In 2024, 68% of Kubernetes clusters still rely on legacy CNI plugins with unpatched CVEs in their encryption layers. WireGuard 2.0 combined with Kubernetes 1.38’s native network policy engine cuts...

item.title: From Zero to Job Data Visualization vs Power BI: Which Wins?
item.description: In Q3 2024, 72% of engineering teams building job data pipelines reported wasted spend on BI tools they couldn't customize—we benchmarked custom viz from zero against Power BI to find the breaking...

item.title: How to Install NVIDIA Drivers on Fedora 44
item.description: This guide covers the RPM Fusion method — the recommended and most maintainable way to install NVIDIA proprietary drivers on Fedora. No manual .run installers, no broken dnf states. Prerequisites...

item.title: Building EDIFlow - Infrastructure Layer: Parsers, Repositories & Data Packages (Part 4)
item.description: Series: Building EDIFlow - A Clean Architecture Journey in TypeScript (Part 4/6) Reading Time: ~12 minutes Recap — Where We Left Off In Part 3, we built the Application Layer — Use Cases, Output...

item.title: SwiftDeploy: A Tool That Writes Its Own Infrastructure
item.description: What is SwiftDeploy? Most DevOps work involves writing config files manually — nginx.conf, docker-compose.yml, environment variables. SwiftDeploy flips this. You describe what you want in one file...

item.title: I built a 200 line AI router in TypeScript. My monthly bill dropped 41%.
item.description: I track my own AI spend across three projects. In March, the line item that grew fastest was not Claude or GPT calls. It was my Cursor seat plus my Copilot seat plus the Anthropic API I was...

item.title: ⚖️ Case File 1.2: The Irreversibility Trap
item.description: Blueprint Felonies Continued.. The most expensive mistake in architecture isn’t making the wrong decision—it’s making it too early. In 17+ years of building enterprise systems, I’ve seen...

item.title: Cloudflare and Stripe just let agents buy domains and ship code. Here is the API.
item.description: On April 30, Cloudflare quietly published a blog post and a docs page that nobody outside the agentic AI crowd noticed for about 24 hours. The headline buried a real shift. AI agents can now...

item.title: Musk’s biggest loyalist became his biggest liability
item.description: I sat down in the Musk v. Altman trial courtroom today, painfully aware that no one was going to ask Shivon Zilis the question on everyone's minds: Girl, what the fuck are you doing? Zilis, who...

item.title: Elon Musk’s Last-Ditch Effort to Control OpenAI: Recruit Sam Altman to Tesla
item.description: Messages between Shivon Zilis and Tesla executives reveal plans in 2017 to start a rival AI lab, potentially led by Altman or Demis Hassabis.

item.title: Nintendo announces a new Star Fox for the Switch 2
item.description: It turns out Fox McCloud's appearance in the Super Mario Galaxy Movie was a tease of things to come: Nintendo just surprise announced the first new Star Fox game in a decade. The game is called,...

item.title: Barry Diller trusts Sam Altman. But ‘trust is irrelevant’ as AGI nears, he says.
item.description: Barry Diller defended OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, while warning that AGI remains an unpredictable force needing guardrails.

item.title: Snap says its $400M deal with Perplexity ‘amicably ended’
item.description: The deal, announced last November, would have seen Perplexity's AI search engine integrated directly into Snapchat.

item.title: Is xAI a neocloud now?
item.description: xAI's real business may be more about building data centers than training AI models.

item.title: Google shuts down Project Mariner
item.description: Google has pulled the plug on Project Mariner, an experimental feature designed to perform tasks for you across the web, as reported earlier by Wired's Maxwell Zeff. The Project Mariner landing...

item.title: Mirai-Based xlabs_v1 Botnet Exploits ADB to Hijack IoT Devices for DDoS Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have exposed a new Mirai-derived botnet that self-identifies as xlabs_v1 and targets internet-exposed devices running Android Debug Bridge (ADB) to enlist them in a...

item.title: How David Sacks crashed and burned in the White House
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter exclusively for Verge subscribers about tech, politics, and Washington intrigue. (It's basically House of Cards, but for nerds.) Not a subscriber yet?...

item.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
The latest Tile Pro is down to $25 — its best price of the year
item.description: If you’re struggling to find a Mother’s Day gift under $30, location trackers are a good investment, helping mom keep tabs on keys, bags, luggage, and other valuables. The latest Tile Pro is one...

item.title: Anthropic Gets in Bed With SpaceX as the AI Race Turns Weird
item.description: In an unexpected turn, the two companies signed a deal for Anthropic to use computing resources from Elon Musk’s xAI.

item.title: How Elon Musk left OpenAI, according to Greg Brockman
item.description: Cutthroat negotiations between startup founders are rarely shared so publicly, especially when a company becomes as world-changing as OpenAI.

item.title: What we’re hearing about the new homepage
item.description: We’ve been reading your feedback on the new homepage. All of it. The kind and encouraging comments, the brutal ones, the “actually this rules,” the “please undo this immediately,” the bug reports,...

item.title: Using AI for Just 10 Minutes Might Make You Lazy and Dumb, Study Shows
item.description: New research suggests that reliance on AI assistants can have a negative impact on people’s ability to think and problem solve.

item.title: Mira Murati tells the court that she couldn’t trust Sam Altman’s words
item.description: Mira Murati, OpenAI's former CTO, has testified under oath that CEO Sam Altman lied to her about the safety standards for a new AI model. In a video deposition shown during the ongoing Musk v....

item.title: The 44 best last-minute Mother’s Day gift ideas for 2026
item.description: Whether it's managing a busy home or looking out for everyone around them, moms spend a lot of time every day caring for others. This Sunday, Mother's Day, is an opportunity to return the favor,...

item.title: SpaceX may spend up to $119B on ‘Terafab’ chip factory in Texas
item.description: The project would be a "multi-phase, next-generation, vertically integrated semiconductor manufacturing and advanced computing fabrication facility," according to the proposal.

item.title: Here’s what Microsoft is offering long-serving employees to voluntarily retire
item.description: Microsoft revealed last month that it's planning to offer long-serving employees in the US the ability to voluntarily retire. While the terms of the buyout were supposed to be announced to...

item.title: DeepSeek could hit $45B valuation from its first investment round
item.description: The Chinese AI lab came to prominence in early 2025 after launching a large language model that trained on a fraction of the compute power and at a fraction of the cost of the big U.S. models like...
